title: salix × pygmaea
intro: Salix × pygmaea, also known as dwarf willow, is a hybrid plant species in the genus Salix, resulting from a cross between Salix herbacea and Salix reticulata. The medicinal properties of Salix × pygmaea include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its salicylic acid content. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce pain and fever, as well as its potential in treating various skin conditions such as acne and eczema. Herbal preparations of Salix × pygmaea can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to topical application or ingested to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
